100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 31, 1926, the British government announced a significant reduction in coal production, aiming to stabilize the struggling industry amid ongoing strikes. In the United States, the Senate debated the merits of the proposed McNary-Haugen Bill, which seeks to support farmers through price stabilization measures. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions escalated as the government faced rising pressures from various factions, highlighting the nation’s tumultuous post-war climate.
